paper_title: Comprehensive Cell-specific Protein Analysis in Early and Late Pollen Development

methods.description: This method describes the process of growing tobacco plants under controlled greenhouse conditions and collecting pollen from flowers for further analysis.

steps.description: Tobacco was grown under greenhouse conditions (12 h of light, 120 mol m Ϫ2 s Ϫ1 , 23°C during the day, 20°C at night, 60% humidity).
steps.searchable_summary: grow tobacco plants under specified greenhouse conditions

steps.description: Flowers of different sizes were collected, and the anthers of individual flowers were sampled in 200 l of 10% mannitol.
steps.searchable_summary: collect flowers and sample anthers in mannitol solution

steps.description: Anthers were gently squeezed open and vortexed, and the supernatant including the released pollen was transferred to a new tube.
steps.searchable_summary: release pollen from anthers and transfer supernatant

steps.description: Pollen was spun down at 100 ϫ g for 1 min and washed twice with 10% mannitol.
steps.searchable_summary: centrifuge and wash pollen with mannitol

steps.description: A subfraction of the pollen of each individual flower was analyzed under a microscope to determine the developmental stage.
steps.searchable_summary: microscopically analyze pollen developmental stage

steps.description: Samples not representing a stage with at least 90% of their pollen were discarded.
steps.searchable_summary: discard samples not meeting stage purity criteria

methods.description: This method involves the preparation and imaging of pollen samples using confocal microscopy to analyze their structure and composition.

steps.description: Pollen samples were fixed in 10% mannitol and 4% formaldehyde overnight, collected via centrifugation, and resuspended in 1 g/ml DAPI and 1% triton X-100 5 min prior to microscopy.
steps.searchable_summary: fix and stain pollen samples for microscopy

steps.description: Images were recorded with an upright point laser scanning confocal microscope (LSM780, Zeiss, Oberkochen, Germany) using a 405-nm diode laser for excitation and a band-pass filter ranging from 450 -550 nm.
steps.searchable_summary: capture images using confocal microscopy

steps.description: Acquired images were processed using Fiji software.
steps.searchable_summary: process microscopy images with Fiji software

methods.description: This method describes the process of analyzing pollen proteins using GeLC-LTQ-Orbitrap mass spectrometry to identify and quantify proteins.

steps.description: Pollen from between 5 and 30 flowers was pooled, freeze-dried, cooled in liquid nitrogen, and ground for 3 min in a shaking mill using three 2-mm steel balls per tube.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare pollen samples by freeze-drying and grinding

steps.description: The pollen fragments were resuspended in 200 l of protein extraction buffer and incubated for 5 min at room temperature.
steps.searchable_summary: resuspend pollen fragments in protein extraction buffer

steps.description: Samples were mixed again by pipetting, incubated for 3 min at 90°C, and then centrifuged at 21,000 ϫ g for 5 min at room temperature.
steps.searchable_summary: incubate and centrifuge pollen samples

steps.description: Supernatants were carefully transferred to a new tube. After the addition of an equal volume of 1.4 M sucrose, proteins were extracted twice with Tris-EDTA buffer-equilibrated phenol.
steps.searchable_summary: extract proteins using sucrose and phenol

steps.description: The combined phenolic phases were counter-extracted with 0.7 M sucrose and subsequently mixed with five volumes of 0.1 M ammonium-acetate in methanol to precipitate the proteins.
steps.searchable_summary: precipitate proteins with ammonium-acetate

steps.description: After 16 h of incubation at Ϫ20°C, samples were centrifuged for 5 min at 5000 ϫ g at 5°C. The pellet was washed twice with 0.1 M ammonium-acetate and once with acetone and then air-dried.
steps.searchable_summary: wash and dry protein pellets

steps.description: Pellets were redissolved in 6 M urea, 5% SDS, and protein concentrations were estimated via bicinchoninic acid assay.
steps.searchable_summary: redissolve protein pellets and estimate concentration

steps.description: Proteins were analyzed via a new gel-LC-MS protocol.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze proteins using gel-LC-MS

methods.method_name: Multivariate Statistical and Bioinformatic Data Analysis
methods.description: This method involves the use of multivariate statistical analyses and bioinformatics tools to analyze proteomic data from pollen samples.

steps.description: Multivariate statistical analyses such as principal components analysis (PCA) and k-means clustering were performed with the statistical toolbox COVAIN.
steps.searchable_summary: perform PCA and k-means clustering using COVAIN

steps.description: Missing values were estimated from the dataset, and data were log transformed before the PCA.
steps.searchable_summary: estimate missing values and log transform data

steps.description: For cluster analysis, the mean NSAF value of each developmental stage was calculated and normalized for each protein.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate and normalize mean NSAF values

steps.description: All proteins in the three used databases were blasted for the closest Arabidopsis homologue using an unpublished Python script.
steps.searchable_summary: blast proteins for Arabidopsis homologues
